channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Haim Yavin, Pioneer of Israeli Television, Passes Away at 93
item.description: Haim Yavin, widely regarded as the father of Israeli television news, has passed away at the age of 93. The veteran broadcaster anchored Israel's first TV news bulletin in 1968 and remained a key figu...

item.title: India offers 1,135 scholarships for Tanzanian students
item.description: India has launched a scholarship program offering 1,135 opportunities for Tanzanian students to pursue higher education. The initiative aims to strengthen bilateral ties between the two nations by sup...

item.title: Police raid leads to deaths of suspects in JPMC doctor murder case
item.description: The Special Investigation Unit (SIU) confirmed that three suspects arrested in connection with the killing of Dr. Akash Kumar, a doctor at Jinnah Postgraduate Medical Centre (JPMC), died in an exchang...

item.title: Iran drone attacks on CIA sites spark US intelligence probe into possible Russian support
item.description: Iranian drones have targeted CIA facilities in the Gulf region, prompting US intelligence agencies to investigate whether Russia provided advanced technology or targeting data for the strikes. Reports...

item.title: Millionaires Push for Higher Taxes on Wealthiest in UK
item.description: A group of 120 wealthy individuals, including former footballer Gary Lineker and music producer Brian Eno, have written an open letter to new Prime Minister Andy Burnham urging a higher tax on the ric...

item.title: Kogi State Compensates Victims of Demolition, Advances Airport Projects
item.description: The Kogi State Government has compensated affected residents after demolishing a structure used by criminals in Idojie-Okene, Okene Local Government Area. Governor Ahmed Usman Ododo, represented by Di...

item.title: Wafcon 2026 Tournament Details and Prize Money Breakdown
item.description: The 2026 Women’s Africa Cup of Nations (Wafcon) is set to kick off in Morocco on July 16, 2026, with 16 teams competing for a total prize pool of R32m. This marks a 100% increase from previous edition...

item.title: South Africa Explains Emergency Hysterectomy Protocols and Patient Rights
item.description: The article discusses emergency hysterectomies as life-saving procedures during childbirth when uncontrollable bleeding occurs. It clarifies that such surgeries are only performed when all other measu...

item.title: US-Saudi Nuclear Deal Conditional on Riyadh Joining Abraham Accords
item.description: US President Donald Trump announced that a landmark nuclear cooperation agreement with Saudi Arabia is contingent on Riyadh joining the Abraham Accords and normalizing relations with Israel. The deal,...

item.title: President Ruto Announces KSh 80 Million for Nairobi Artisans and Mechanics to Upgrade Equipment
item.description: President William Ruto has pledged KSh 80 million to support Nairobi's informal sector artisans and mechanics by providing modern tools and equipment. Over 5,000 workers attended a meeting at State Ho...

item.title: Review: The Odyssey's Music by Ludwig Göransson
item.description: The article critiques the film score for Christopher Nolan's *The Odyssey*, composed by Ludwig Göransson. While the score incorporates ancient instruments and raw, bleak sounds to mirror the film's se...

item.title: Parents of Australian doctor who joined ISIS speak about shock after son found alive in Iraq
item.description: The parents of Tareq Kamleh, an Australian doctor who joined ISIS and was believed dead, have spoken about their devastation after learning he is alive and imprisoned in Iraq. The Red Cross contacted ...

item.title: The Hidden Cost of Constant Agreement: Why Avoiding Conflict May Harm Your Peace
item.description: The article discusses how blind agreement and avoidance of conflict, as highlighted in the book 'The Most Truthful Book of Lies', can lead to internal turmoil rather than peace. It critiques societal ...

item.title: Evangelicals' political influence grows as pastors enter Côte d’Ivoire's political arena
item.description: The article explores the growing political involvement of evangelical Christians in Côte d’Ivoire, where religious leaders are increasingly entering politics. It highlights the case of Stéphane Kipré,...

item.title: President Marcos Inspects Injured Navy Personnel Following Ayungin Shoal Incident
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. recently visited two injured Navy personnel stationed at the Ayungin Shoal, a disputed territory in the South China Sea, to assess their condition and offer support. The...

item.title: CNN Host Reacts to Debate on Congressional Dating Rules and Workplace Culture
item.description: CNN host Audie Cornish and her panelists discussed House Speaker Mike Johnson's push to ban romantic relationships between members of Congress and Capitol Hill staffers. The conversation followed two ...

item.title: Zenith Bank wins Africa’s Best Bank award for 2nd year in a row
item.description: Zenith Bank Plc has been named Africa’s Best Bank and Nigeria’s Best Bank for the second consecutive year at the Euromoney Awards for Excellence 2026, reinforcing its position as one of Africa’s leadi...

item.title: Premiers Seek Tariff Strategy from Carney Amid U.S. Trade Tensions
item.description: Canada's premiers are meeting with Prime Minister Mark Carney in Charlottetown, Prince Edward Island, to discuss strategies for negotiating with U.S. President Donald Trump amid escalating trade tensi...

item.title: Priyanka Gandhi questions government response to student protests and demands accountability
item.description: Congress MP Priyanka Gandhi Vadra has criticised Prime Minister Narendra Modi over the government's handling of ongoing student protests related to alleged NEET paper leaks and other concerns. Speakin...

item.title: Kenya Dental Association Condemns KMPDC's Plan to Allow Unqualified Practitioners
item.description: The Kenya Dental Association (KDA) has strongly condemned the Kenya Medical Practitioners and Dentists Council (KMPDC) for publishing a scope of practice that could allow unqualified individuals to of...
